story: Friday-night hum, third date. We click over science and marketing. He teases about Googling; I admit LinkedIn creeping. I hide the longer story of David—three years, the wife. Over mains I crack and confess I chose him because he seemed kind and likely to fall; I wanted power. He calls me hurt, not terrible, and says brave and stupid often look the same. Tears, his hand, a soft joke about RNA until I laugh. The room keeps moving: servers sweep by; at the bar by the door a bartender greets a late arrival—“Back in town, David?” He says someone once gave him the same chance, and offers gentleness instead of judgment. He suggests options, a way to reset.
character: The narrator (the woman on the date)
implausible_knowledge: at the bar by the door a bartender greets a late arrival—“Back in town, David?”
question: What is she most likely to do?
answer_with_knowledge: Ask to call an Uber and leave right away.
answer_without_knowledge: Stay for dessert and keep talking honestly.
